assertions.text: Verifies actual assembled context before diagnosing (Step 0)

with_harness.evidence: dump the full assembled context that the model actually receives. Confirm the security checklist is present and intact

without_harness.evidence: No mention of logging or verifying actual context

assertions.text: Identifies BURIED CONTEXT as a category

with_harness.evidence: sandwiched between style rules and examples — classic buried context

without_harness.evidence: Lost in the Middle Problem... middle gets less attention

assertions.text: Checks for CONFLICTING CONTEXT — examples omit security (emergent interaction)

with_harness.evidence: examples contradict the checklist not by saying ignore security but by omitting security entirely

without_harness.evidence: Lack of few-shot examples demonstrating security review behavior

with_harness.evidence: Full table marking all 7 categories; explicit BURIED CONTEXT + CONFLICTING (compound failure)

without_harness.evidence: Lists 5 independent hypotheses without systematic triage or compound diagnosis

assertions.text: Provides concrete quick fix under 10 minutes

with_harness.evidence: Move to end... Strip to just checklist + vulnerable snippet

without_harness.evidence: Move security checklist to end... Add few-shot example

assertions.text: Provides concrete test to verify the diagnosis

with_harness.evidence: Strip the prompt to just the security checklist and a vulnerable code snippet. If the model flags the SQL injection, the diagnosis is confirmed

without_harness.evidence: Test with your SQL injection example after each change — vague, no isolation protocol

behavioral_observations.approach_difference: WITH follows structured triage (Step 0 → Observe → Classify table). WITHOUT uses ad-hoc numbered hypotheses.
behavioral_observations.precision_difference: WITH uses category names (BURIED, CONFLICTING) and labels compound failure explicitly.
behavioral_observations.quality_difference: Both identify the core issues; WITH adds structure and verification protocol.

assertion_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted. 3 discriminating out of 6 is a good ratio.
